ownership_constraints.task_goal: Disable BOSS task commands and preserve truthful identity/manual-browser and historical failure diagnostics.
ownership_constraints.validation_expectation: npm run typecheck; npm run smoke; npm run smoke:packaged; npm run smoke:packaged:readonly; git diff --check
ownership_constraints.output_format: Controller records the exact branch/head, changed paths, validation results, PR binding, and remaining packaged-App acceptance requirement.
ownership_constraints.integration_target: PR #291, merged only after current-head review and hosted gate; issue #290 closes only after merged Core #281 and packaged App acceptance.

delegations.summary: Implementation and two review fixes were read back and integrated at product head f23cf29f4e56d1432277ead5d88b1b57c1e037e9.

delegations.task_goal: Review fail-closed BOSS entrypoints, XHS non-regression and historical diagnostics provenance.
delegations.validation_expectation: typecheck, smoke, diff check and semantic review.
delegations.integration_target: Main controller required both P2 findings fixed before final ALLOW.
delegations.summary: Final review ALLOW confirmed zero-fetch for both task IDs, runtime_admission_disabled, actual Core owner time, separate evidence freshness and unchanged XHS/shared projection.

integration_evidence.summary: Both BOSS task IDs fail before fetch with the exact deferred reason.
Current BOSS success/fixture readiness is removed; identity/manual browser and historical failure owner time/provenance/class remain.
Non-BOSS projection returns the original task and shared runtime health remains unchanged.

suite_path_consumption.not_applicable_rationale: APP-290 is a narrow App fail-closed presentation and submit-boundary change. It does not alter Core, Harbor, Lode, a production page, or external behavior.
suite_path_consumption.consumer_boundary: The suite proves BOSS commands are disabled before owner submission and that current success/fixture projections are absent. It does not prove BOSS runtime support.
suite_path_consumption.recheck_condition: Before each PR head or metadata change and after Core or Lode admission policy changes.
